steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that TRAPPIST planets are tidally locked, meaning each planet's orbital period equals its rotational period. The correct answer must apply this relationship specifically to TRAPPIST-1e using data from the table.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — [confuses planet radius with rotational period]: Radius is a size measurement, not a time measurement — it cannot equal an orbital period. ✗
  • Choice B — [uses data from a different planet]: The orbital period cited does not correspond to TRAPPIST-1e in the table. ✗
  • Choice C — 'TRAPPIST-1e has an orbital period of 6.1 days, so its rotational period is also 6.1 days': This correctly pulls TRAPPIST-1e's orbital period from the table and applies the passage's tidal-locking relationship. ✓
  • Choice D — [uses data from a different planet]: The values given belong to another TRAPPIST planet, not TRAPPIST-1e. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
